sections.paragraph:
περιτέμνω 2 aor. περιέτεμον; pf. περιτέτμηκα LXX. Pass.: 1 fut. περιτμηθήσομαι; 1 aor. περιετμήθην; pf. 3 sg. περιτέτμηται (Just., D. 28, 4); ptc. περιτετμημένος LXX; plpf. 3 sg. περιετέτμητο (Just., D. 46, 3) (Hom. et al.) ‘to cut off around’; in our lit. and the LXX, somet. fig., only in the sense: to cut off the foreskin of the male genital organ, circumcise (so somet. as act., somet. as mid. [‘circumcise oneself’], since Hdt. 2, 36, 2; 2, 104, 1 [of the Egyptians and several other peoples], also Diod S 1, 28, 3; 1, 55, 5; 3, 32, 4 [Egyptians, Colchians, Ἰουδαῖοι]; Ptolemaeus, Περὶ Ἡρώδου τ. βασιλέως [I a.d.]: 199 Fgm. 1 Jac.; Strabo 17, 2, 5; Philo Bybl. [c. 100 a.d.]: 790 Fgm. 2, 33 p. 812, 8 Jac. [in Eus., PE 1, 10, 33]; Sallust. 9 p. 18, 17; PLond I, 24, 12f p. 32 [163 b.c.]; PTebt 291, 33; 292, 7; 20; 293, 12; 14; 19; Mitt-Wilck. I/2, 77 I, 11; III, 11 [149 a.d.]; OdeSol 11:2; TestLevi 6:3; Philo Alex.; Joseph.; Just.) in our lit. prob. only in act. and pass.

ⓐ lit., w. acc. of pers. Lk 1:59; 2:21 (s. Schwyzer II 372); J 7:22; Ac 7:8; 15:5; 16:3; 21:21; B 9:8 (Gen 17:23ff). Pass. be circumcised, have oneself circumcised (Orig., C. Cels. 1, 22, 4; Did., Gen. 75, 6; B-D-F §314) Ac 15:1, 24 v.l.; 1 Cor 7:18b; Gal 2:3 (Ptolemaeus, Περὶ Ἡρώδου τ. βασιλέως: 199 Jac. [I a.d.] Ἰδουμαῖοι ἀναγκασθέντες περιτέμνεσθαι. S. SBelkin, JBL 54, ’35, 43–47); 5:2f; 6:12, 13b. οἱ περιτεμνόμενοι those who have themselves circumcised vs. 13a. περιτετμημένος circumcised, in the state of being circumcised 1 Cor 7:18a; Gal 6:13a v.l.

ⓑ fig.

α. of baptism περιετμήθητε περιτομῇ ἀχειροποιήτῳ Col 2:11 (OCullmann, D. Tauflehre des NT ’48, 50–63).

β. Barnabas maintains strongly that the scripture does not require a physical circumcision: περιέτεμεν ἡμῶν τὴν καρδίαν 9:1a. The κύριος says περιτμήθητε τὰς καρδίας ὑμῶν vs. 1b. Obviously Jer 4:4 (cp. Dt 10:16; OdeSol 11:2) is meant; B comes closer to it in περιτμήθητε τῷ κυρίῳ ὑμῶν let yourselves be circumcised for your Lord 9:5a, and in the explanation of it περιτμήθητε τὴν σκληροκαρδίαν ὑμῶν vs. 5b. What is true of the heart is also true of the ears περιέτεμεν ἡμῶν τὰς ἀκοάς 9:4. Cp. 10:12. In 9:6 it is acknowledged that circumcision is somet. justified thus: περιτέτμηται ὁ λαὸς εἰς σφραγῖδα, and it is explained that Ἀβραὰμ ἐν πνεύματι προβλέψας εἰς τὸν Ἰησοῦν περιέτεμεν vs. 7.—Schürer I 536ff–40; Dssm., B 149ff [BS 151–3]; UWilcken, HGunkel and PWendland, APF 2, 1903, 4–31; WOtto, Priester u. Tempel im hellenist. Ägypten I 1905, 213ff; JMatthes, De Besnijdenis: Teylers Theol. Tijdschrift 6, 1908, 163–91; FDölger, Sphragis 1911, 51ff; Billerb. IV 1928, 23–40; FBryk, D. Beschneidung b. Mann u. Weib ’31; JSasson, JBL 85, ’66, 473–76; JLieu, Circumcision, Women, and Salvation: NTS 40, ’94, 358–70.—RAC II, 159–69; BHHW I 223–25; TRE V 714–24. New Docs 3, 81; M-M. EDNT. TW.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

ptc. ptc. = participle

plpf. plpf. = pluperfect

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

somet. somet. = sometimes

act. act. = active

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

Strabo Strabo , I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Philo Bybl. Philo Bybl. = P. of Byblos, hist., c. 100 a.d.—List 5

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Sallust Sallust , IV a.d.—List 5

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

Mitt-Wilck. Mitt-Wilck. = LMitteis/UWilcken, Grundzüge und Chrestomathie der Papyruskunde—Lists 4, 6

OdeSol OdeSol = Gk. Ode of Solomon 11: PBodmer XI; III a.d.—List 2

TestLevi TestLevi = Testament of Levi, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

prob. prob. = probable, probably

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

acc. acc. = accusative

pers. pers. = person(s)

Schwyzer Schwyzer = ES., Griechische Grammatik—List 6

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

ff ff = (and) following

Orig Orig , var. works, II–III a.d.—List 5

Did. Did. = Didymus Caecus (the Blind), commentator on OT books, IV a.d.—List 5

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

JBL JBL = Journal of Biblical Literature—List 6

NT NT = New Testament

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Dssm. Dssm. = ADeissmann, various works—List 6

BS BS , s. Dssm., BS—List 6

APF APF = Archiv für Papyrusforschung—Lists 4, 6

Billerb. Billerb. = HLStrack and PBillerbeck, Kommentar z. NT aus Talmud u. Midrasch—List 6

NTS NTS = New Testament Studies—List 6

RAC RAC = Reallexikon für Antike u. Christentum—List 6

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

TRE TRE = Theologische Realenzyklopädie—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

EDNT EDNT = Exegetical Dictionary of the NT—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 806
